title: Goodstack — Security controls across the SDLC
subtitle: As few vulnerabilities reach production as possible. Controls flow left → right from cheap, early prevention toward runtime detection & response. Cards are colour-coded by risk-reduction impact.

controls.desc: Threat model new features / design reviews so we design vulns out before code exists.
Secrets scanning at pre-commit and in CI.
npm audit for high/critical supply chain vulns.
PGP-sign commits to ensure they came from a trusted laptop, else fail.
Secrets scanning at pre-commit and in CI.
npm audit for high/critical supply chain vulns.
Dependabot scanning for supply chain vulns (medium frequency, 2×/day).
SAST scanning — parses code into an AST then checks for theoretical vulns.
tfsec / checkov / Trivy-config gate on Terraform.
Generate and publish SBOM so that we can check for vulns post-production.
Code signing in CI, then verify the signature in production to ensure what we built is what runs.
Daily ECR container scanning to look for vulnerable packages.
DAST scanning of API and frontend for obvious vulns.
AWS WAF on CloudFront/ALB — managed rule groups (Core, Known-Bad-Inputs, SQLi, IP-reputation, Anonymous-IP) + rate-based rule.
Verify the CI signature with a pre-start hook to ensure what we built is what runs.
SBOM checking for newly disclosed vulns (medium frequency, 2×/day).
Athena saved queries over ALB/CloudFront logs (404/403 sweeps, hits on /.env /.git /admin, SQLi/XSS in params, single-IP spikes, oversized responses, 5xx spikes).
GuardDuty ECS Runtime Monitoring for compromise in running containers.
VPC Flow / DNS / CloudTrail; alert on secret-scan hits in history.
Quarterly exploratory pen testing using paid humans.
Weekly AWS Prowler scans of our infra.
Weekly pen test using AWS Security Agent.
